1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100011872) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MCCARTNEY, ERIKA AS TO DEFENDANT WEBER-STEPHEN PRODUCTS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY WSP HOLDINGS CO, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 100013989) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MCCARTNEY, ERIKA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF STIPULATED CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE SETTLEMENT DOES NOT APPEAR TO SO FAR OUT OF THE BALLPARK OF OTHER PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENTS THAT HAVE BEEN APPROVED NOTWITHSTANDING THE HIGH EFFECTIVE HOURLY RATE FOR THE FEES PAID TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND A CIVIL PENALTY ONLY 25% OF THE FEES. IN OTHER SITUATIONS THESE OR SIMILAR SETTLEMENT TERMS MIGHT BE GROUNDS TO ASK THE PARTIES RENEGOTIATE THEIR TERMS, BUT THE COURT CHOOSES NOT TO MAKE THAT REQUEST IN THIS CASE. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL NEED TO PROVIDE A PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED JUDGMENT, NOT A SINGLE COMBINED PROPOSED ORDER AND JUDGMENT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Off Calendar

Joinder In Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WEBER-STEPHEN PRODUCTS LLC AND WSP HOLDINGS CO.'S Joinder In Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Consent Judgment is off calendar in light of the ruling on the motion sought to be joined. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY'S Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Consent Judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement does not appear to so far out of the ballpark of other Proposition 65 settlements that have been approved notwithstanding the high effective hourly rate for the fees paid to plaintiff's counsel and a civil penalty only 25% of the fees. In other situations these or similar settlement terms might be grounds to ask the parties renegotiate their terms, but the court chooses not to make that request in this case. Counsel for plaintiff will need to provide a proposed order and proposed judgment, not a single combined proposed order and judgment. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
